content:
You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the zephyr codebase.
Zephyr is a scalable real-time operating system (RTOS) for connected, resource-constrained devices. It includes device drivers, HAL, networking (BLE, Wi-Fi, Thread), USB, shell, logging, and supports multiple architectures (ARM, RISC-V, x86, Xtensa). Code is primarily C.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
You will be given a PR description. Implement the described changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Add Watchdog Feeding Around Potentially Long-Running Sensor Operations in STM32 DCMI Driver

### Summary
This PR addresses a potential watchdog timeout issue in the STM32 DCMI video driver. The `video_stm32_dcmi_set_fmt` function calls `video_set_format` and `video_estimate_fmt_size`, which may involve I2C communication or other time-consuming operations with the image sensor. These operations can take longer than the watchdog timeout period, especially in systems with aggressive watchdog configurations.

The fix adds `sys_watchdog_feed()` calls before and after the sensor-related `video_set_format` call to ensure the watchdog timer is serviced during these potentially long-running operations. This prevents premature watchdog resets while maintaining the driver's functionality.

### Changes
- `drivers/video/video_stm32_dcmi.c`: Added `sys_watchdog_feed()` calls before and after the `video_set_format()` invocation in `video_stm32_dcmi_set_fmt()` to prevent watchdog timeouts during sensor configuration.

### Testing
- Verified compilation with STM32 DCMI driver enabled.
- Confirmed no functional changes; driver behavior remains the same but with added watchdog protection.
- Tested on STM32H743-based board with OV2640 sensor; no watchdog resets observed during format setting operations.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/drivers/video/video_stm32_dcmi.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>static int video_stm32_dcmi_set_fmt(const struct device *dev, struct video_format *fmt)
{
	const struct video_stm32_dcmi_config *config = dev->config;
	struct video_stm32_dcmi_data *data = dev->data;
	int ret;

	ret = video_set_format(config->sensor_dev, fmt);
	if (ret < 0) {
		return ret;
	}

	ret = video_estimate_fmt_size(fmt);
	if (ret < 0) {
		return ret;
	}

	data->fmt = *fmt;

	return 0;
}</old_str>
<new_str>static int video_stm32_dcmi_set_fmt(const struct device *dev, struct video_format *fmt)
{
	const struct video_stm32_dcmi_config *config = dev->config;
	struct video_stm32_dcmi_data *data = dev->data;
	int ret;

	/* Feed watchdog before potentially long-running sensor operation */
	sys_watchdog_feed();

	ret = video_set_format(config->sensor_dev, fmt);
	if (ret < 0) {
		return ret;
	}

	/* Feed watchdog after sensor operation and before format size estimation */
	sys_watchdog_feed();

	ret = video_estimate_fmt_size(fmt);
	if (ret < 0) {
		return ret;
	}

	data->fmt = *fmt;

	return 0;
}</new_str>
